item.title: Error:
item.content:encoded:
In original text: "(…)which was believed to be owned by Marshall, Carter and Dark LLP."

Shouldn't it be Ltd.?

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article has been removed due to it non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). The image originates from here and its license contains "NC", making it non-compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure if this was the intended interpretation, although the comments by the author make me think it wasn't, but my personal interpretation of the article is that

+ show block
– hide block

The maker of the object knew exactly what they were doing when they made the horses produce petroleum. The horses were then used as intended, resulting in the large oil reserves in the Middle East. Centuries later, western powers fulfilled Valerian's curse by destabilizing the region in pursuit of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a neutral vote for me. I think the concept is meh, but I think the document really saves this from getting downvoted. Particularly, this quote:

"A force to wipe out the Persian dogs. To make barren their lands, and sickly their people. A force to destroy every square foot on which they have ever walked and to plague their kingdom with bloodshed for millennia."

This was really clever, given what the object does. Although this does not save the article for me, though. It feels very bare bones to be perfectly honest. I think that if a little bit more time was put in it would be a lot better. Like, maybe some more correspondents between the author of the letter and the emperor or something along those lines.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Practical in what way? Sure now petroleum is a hugely valuable global commodity, and that is reflected in some my team's other articles (which were all written after this scip, btw), but in the context of this article it's just a black oily substance intolerable to life. The fact that oil is valuable is in no way touched upon in this article, and doesn't actually play any part in the story.

If you don't like the article, that's totally your prerogative, and I don't have a problem with you voting however you'd like, but I just don't really understand this particular point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gonna have to read the MCD doc to see if there's something which makes this more interesting and turns my vote into a neutral one. But right now the given backstory, whilst not necessarily bad per se, just feels rather incongrous with the object itself; it feels just too practical that the chosen cursed substance just so happens to be the much sought petroleum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm thinking:

"In addition to SCP-2463, MTF-M3 also recovered a Marshall, Carter and Dark for the object."

should be be something like:

" In addition to SCP-2463, MTF-M3 also recovered a Marshall, Carter and Dark document for the object." (or something along those lines)

I kind of like both this and the MC&D version. I like when there are two perspectives on an item (foundation and goi, or two gois, etc).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the MC&D version better, and this doesn't really add anything to the info that presented.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This ends up being a fairly barebones description of an anomaly that you already told me about. The MC&D format tells a better story with the changing value of this over time as the world changed around it.

Even if this was first, I think it may have been a mistake to have two documents about the exact same information.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can empathise with that, since my contest entry was inspired directly from a Tale TobiasTheTapir did for the contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: True story! I did do a fair amount of homework for the history of the item. But the anomaly itself, and the 3rd century origin, were DrSolo's before I signed on to the team. I definitely couldn't have done what I did without springboarding off of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is a typo - Marshall is written as "Marsall".

Can't really find any issues other than tiny nitpicking. Time to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
roughly 250 CE

It should say "circa 250 CE" just a small mistake I noticed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be fair, this was completely Dr Solo's work and made this far before Boogeyman wrote the MC&D entries; he basically provided all the framework for Boogeyman to use.

I think Solo did a fantastic job thinking up this concept and executed it very well. Stands up on its own very well as an SCP, imo. Will definitely upvote after contest is over

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My greatest concern with this article is that this and the goi-format talk about the same anomaly. This forces me to compare which looks better to express their respective story. And I will have to admit that MC&D pulled it off better. Sure we got the quantitative details and historic anecdotes here, but MC&D gave a more impressive showing in their history (and quantifying is not the affairs of businessmen).

Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Biting the bullet and putting up my teams SCP. I know one of the crosslinks is broken, it will be remedied once my team's last piece goes up, and the article is supposed to work without it anyway.

Written as part of a collaboration between myself, SinfulSyntax, DrStranger, and Boogey_Man23 for the MTF 2016 contest.

All the historical stuff is legitimate, though if you have any questions send 'em my way.
